message:
fix(security): bound challenge and nonce storage with FIFO eviction, harden SSRF against trailing-dot numeric aliases

challenge_flow.rs:
- Add MAX_CHALLENGES (4096) capacity limit on the challenge map.
- Implement evict_terminal_challenges() using BTreeMap key-order
  iteration to remove the oldest Denied/Promoted challenges first,
  preventing unbounded memory growth from long-running controllers.
- Call eviction after every new challenge insertion.

revocation_freshness_gate.rs:
- Add MAX_CONSUMED_NONCES (65,536) cap with FIFO eviction using a
  VecDeque insertion-order queue alongside the BTreeSet. Without this,
  an adversary controlling token creation could exhaust memory by
  verifying many unique-nonce proofs across a long epoch.
- BTreeSet-only eviction is insufficient because it evicts by
  lexicographic order, allowing crafted nonces to push out recent
  entries. The VecDeque ensures oldest-first temporal eviction.
- Add tests: bounded capacity assertion and duplicate-nonce queue
  stability verification.

ssrf_policy.rs:
- Add tests for trailing-dot hex (0x7f000001.), octal (0177.0.0.1.),
  and shorthand (127.1.) numeric IP aliases, verifying they are
  correctly classified as private IPs and rejected by the SSRF policy.

policy_checkpoint.rs:
- Improve test readability by extracting trace IDs into named
  variables (create_trace_id, append_trace_id) instead of bare "t".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
